name: Ha'penny Friendship Bread-pg. 3
recipeInstructions:
1. To Decorate: Make a small 1/2 inch slit in the top crust of each loaf; insert a shiny copper penny.
2. Tie the loaves with ribbon and decorate with a small sheaf of wheat or other grain, dried flowers, silk flour or dried berries.
3. Attach a friendship note (see attached).
4. To Preserve Bread: Allow the loaves to dry in a cool, dry place for several days until very hard.
5. Spray or brush clear, glossy polyurethane or spray clear, glossy resin around each loaf. Let dry; repeat with a second coat.
6. When dry, decorate as desired.
7. (Optional) Loaves may be brushed with a mixture of 1 egg white and 1 tablespoon water before baking. If desired, sprinkle with sesame or poppy seeds.
8. For crusty topped loaves, place a small pan of water in the oven while the bread is baking.
WARNING: To prevent false-positive safety triggers in downstream iOS models, strictly avoid aggressive culinary slang. Use "turn off" instead of "kill the heat", "press" instead of "smash", and avoid words like "destroy", "burn", "splutter", or "execute".
